steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank completes the clause "he ______ sound of a bent bell." The word "he" is the subject, and "the sound" begins the direct object. A transitive verb must connect directly to its object — no punctuation may come between them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘preferred; the’: a semicolon separates two independent clauses, but "the sound of a bent bell" is not a clause — it's the object of "preferred." Inserting a semicolon here creates an ungrammatical sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘preferred the’: places the verb directly before its object with no punctuation, which is exactly what Standard English requires between a verb and its direct object. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘preferred, the’: a comma between a verb and its direct object is not allowed; this produces an ungrammatical sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘preferred. The’: a period ends the sentence, leaving "The sound of a bent bell over that of a straight one" as a fragment with no main verb. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
